Programs & For Schools

The Foundation's
work on the ground.

The James For Change Foundation doesn't just show up and leave. The work is designed to be implemented in schools, in legal systems, in communities, so that what James brings becomes something your institution can sustain.

Program 01

The Horizon Initiative

Helping Students See Farther, So They Can Live Better

Prevention is the highest form of public safety. The Horizon Initiative is a school-based student development program that supports middle and high school students in building decision-making skills, personal responsibility, and future planning capacity.

Delivered through structured small-group sessions called Legacy Labs, the program gives students a consistent, facilitated space to strengthen critical thinking, leadership behaviors, and positive peer engagement. This is a sustained partnership model, not a one-time assembly.

What this includes
→Legacy Lab small-group sessions (8–12 students, weekly, 12-week cycle)
→In-school presentations and assemblies
→Student mentorship and Q&A sessions
→Prevention education and early-intervention programming
Bring James to Your School
Program 02

Reentry Legal Assistance

Leaving incarceration doesn't mean leaving the system behind. The Foundation connects returning citizens with legal resources, guidance, and direct assistance: the kind James himself provided during law school and has continued ever since.

From record restrictions to pardon applications to restoration of rights, the Foundation navigates the legal landscape so individuals can focus on rebuilding.

Services & support
→Record restriction guidance and applications
→Pardon application assistance
→Restoration of rights support
→Connection to legal resources and referrals
→Educational and employment pathway support
Get in Touch
Program 03

BASICS · State Bar of Georgia

As Chair of the BASICS Committee of the State Bar of Georgia, James leads statewide initiatives that connect individuals with education, entrepreneurial development, and reentry support systems.

BASICS represents the institutionalization of James' personal mission: creating infrastructure within the legal profession itself to serve communities that the system has historically overlooked.

Committee focus areas
→Statewide programming and initiatives
→Education and workforce development for underserved communities
→Entrepreneurial insights and opportunity development
→Policy advocacy within the legal community
Partner With the Foundation
For Schools & Districts

Beyond the assembly.

Speaking brings people in. But the Foundation is built for districts and schools that want something they can actually implement: recurring programming, structured curriculum, and a sustained presence that moves the needle over time.

Coweta County Schools
James White III & Dr. Gamal Kemp
Asst. Superintendent, Coweta County Schools
Partnership in Action
"Prevention is the highest form of public safety."

James partners directly with school district leaders to build sustained programming — not one-time visits, but lasting infrastructure that keeps students engaged and on track.

Tier 1
Single Visit

One presentation, assembly, or classroom visit. A strong introduction for students who haven't had access to this kind of voice.

45–90 min · In-person or virtual
Tier 2
Workshop Series

3–5 structured sessions built around a theme: rights, reentry, education, or law as a path. Goes deeper than a single visit and builds relationships with students over time.

Multi-session · Flexible scheduling
Tier 3 · Most Popular
Semester Program

A structured semester-long engagement with regular touchpoints, curriculum integration, and measurable outcomes. Designed for schools that want real change, not a check-in-a-box.

Full semester · Curriculum-integrated
Tier 4
District Partnership

A full-year, district-wide engagement model. Multiple schools, coordinated programming, staff training, and Foundation resources embedded in the district's prevention strategy.

Full year · District-wide
Start a Partnership Conversation

Some tiers are available at reduced or no cost depending on school context. Reach out and we'll find a fit.
